utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: next patient is grace ross . date of birth 8-23-97 . ms. ross is a new patient . she's here today for a full spectrum std testing . the patient states that she's having symptoms of discomfort , a change in discharge , and odor in her vaginal region . she says that the right side of her vagina is starting to become painful . she denies any fevers or chills . she reports , that she recently learned , her partner has been in sexual encounters with other people , and she wants to be evaluated for all stds .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hello , ms. ross . i'm dr. diaz . it's good to meet you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi . yes . nice to meet you , too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , how are you doing today ? i hear that you're here for std testing . is that correct ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . actually , i found out earlier this week , that my boyfriend has been cheating on me , for some time . i'm really worried , that i could have caught something , you know ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i wanted to make sure i do n't have anything .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . i'm sorry to hear that . of course , we can do that today . um . well , let's see . how do you feel ? do you have any symptoms ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , um , i started noticing some weird discharge last week .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , it smells different . it's not the same color , or consistency , as usually , it is .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: right .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um . that part did n't even phase me , until i noticed that the right side of my vagina was hurting . just sore , you know , but that has n't gone away .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yep . nah , that does sound like a cause for concern . how long have you been with him ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmm , we've been dating for three years .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: as far as , i can figure , he started cheating on me , i think , about three months ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh- . um , okay . do you ... have you ever had an irregular pap smear ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , yeah , i did , but it was back whenever i was 20 or so . it has n't ... and i have n't had one since .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and when was your last pap smear ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , about six months ago . it came back normal , though .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . good , okay . well , let's see ... let's take a look at you , and then , we'll get some samples for std testing . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you'll hear me talk through your exam , so that i can get it all documented . uh- ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: use my general physical exam template . pelvic exam demonstrates no external lesions . normal labia majora and labia minora . normal physiological discharge , with normal color and smell .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: ms. ross , i'm going to touch you , now . you'll feel my fingers , and the speculum in your vagina . you'll feel some pressure , now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: cervix is normal . vaginal wall is normal . no cervical motion tenderness and no adnexal tenderness or masses noted . swabs were done for std testing .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , ms. ross . everything looks good from a visual standpoint . i'm going to send off std testing for , uh , gonorrhea , chlamydia , trichomoniasis , and i'll have you give some blood for syphilis , hiv and hepatitis c. some of these tests will take longer than others . we will call you , if we see anything , but if you have n't heard anything , you can call us in three days for results .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . sounds good . thank you , so much .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: of course , my pleasure . and , if everything is normal , i'll see you again , at your next annual checkup .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: assessment : is screen for std .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: plan : discussed with patient , and please add that she is high risk due to an abnormal pap smear in the past , and the recent discovery of her sexual partner having multiple partners , for some period of time .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: end of recording .

detail_list.summary: Ms. Ross is a 23-year-old female presenting as a new patient for full-spectrum STD testing.
The patient reports vaginal discomfort, abnormal discharge, odor, and right-sided vaginal pain, while denying fever or chills.
The doctor asks if the patient is here for STD testing.
The doctor asks if the patient is experiencing any symptoms.
The patient reports noticing abnormal vaginal discharge starting last week.
The patient reports the discharge has a different odor, color, and consistency than usual.
The patient reports persistent soreness and pain on the right side of her vagina.
The doctor asks how long the patient has been with her partner.
The patient has been dating her partner for three years.
The doctor asks if the patient has ever had an irregular Pap smear.
The patient had an abnormal Pap smear around age 20.
The doctor asks when the patient's last Pap smear was.
The patient's last Pap smear was six months ago and was normal.
Pelvic exam shows no external lesions, normal labia majora and minora, and normal physiological discharge.
Cervix and vaginal wall are normal; no cervical motion tenderness, adnexal tenderness, or masses noted; swabs obtained for STD testing.
The doctor will order tests for gonorrhea, chlamydia, trichomoniasis, syphilis, HIV, and hepatitis C, and instructs the patient to call for results in three days.
The doctor will see the patient again at her next annual checkup if results are normal.
